address: 120 W. 1st Street (corner of W 1st St and N Market St), Milan, MO 63556
audience: General public including seniors; farmers market draws older adult shoppers in small rural communities
tabling_details: Weekly outdoor farmers market with multiple vendors selling produce and goods. Vendor spots available; contact Sullivan County Health Department at (660) 265-4141 for vendor inquiries. Market runs Thursdays 2:30-6pm (also reported as 3pm-6pm) May through October.
notes: Weekly recurring market, Thursdays, May through October. First occurrence in >=21-day window: July 23, 2026. Last Thursday in 120-day window: Oct 22, 2026. Start date corrected from prior run (prior entry started July 2, only 4 days out).

venue: East John Street near Twin Parks
audience: General community; seniors are a significant portion of shoppers at small-town Missouri farmers markets
tabling_details: Open vendor market — vendors must have homegrown or homemade items and be set up by 3:00 PM. Thursdays 3-6 PM. Also a Saturday market at Prenger's Foods parking lot (542 S Main St) year-round 8am-noon.
notes: confirmed
column22: Operates Thursdays 3-6 PM, May through October. First occurrence in >=21-day window: July 23, 2026. Last Thursday in 120-day window: Oct 22, 2026. Rural small-town farmers markets in MO consistently draw 65+ shoppers. Start date corrected from prior run (prior entry started July 2, only 4 days out).

audience: General public including seniors; SNAP/EBT accepted indicating broad community reach
tabling_details: 35+ vendors participate each market day; vendor applications accepted — call 660-263-6643 x 212 or email farmers@randolphcountyhealth.org
notes: Held every other Thursday, 4-7 pm, from May 28 through October 1, 2026. First occurrence in >=21-day window: July 23. Biweekly dates in window: July 23, Aug 6, Aug 20, Sep 3, Sep 17, Oct 1. Accepts SNAP/EBT with Double Up Food Bucks matching. Start date corrected from prior run (prior entry started July 9, only 11 days out).

audience: Antique, heritage, and collectible enthusiasts; audience strongly skews 55+/senior as the event celebrates late 19th and early 20th century machinery and collectibles
tabling_details: Vendor information page exists at macon-flywheel.com/vendor-information; event includes a large flea market with vendor spaces; contact macon.flywheel@gmail.com for vendor details
cost: $10 admission for all 4 days; kids 13 and under free
notes: July 23-26, 2026 dates confirmed via search results. 40-acre Macon County Park features antique steam engines, tractors, gas engines, machinery, cars, trucks, large flea market, live music, museum tours, food. Audience strongly skews older/senior due to antique/heritage focus.

venue: North Elson and East Washington Streets
address: N. Elson St. & E. Washington St.
audience: General community shoppers; farmers markets consistently attract 55+ shoppers seeking fresh produce and local goods
tabling_details: Vendor spots available through the Kiwanis Club of Kirksville; market operates every Saturday May through October 7am-12pm. Vendor applications through market management.
notes: Recurring weekly market every Saturday, May through October, 7:00am-12:00pm. First Saturday in >=21-day window: July 25, 2026. Last Saturday in 120-day window: Oct 24, 2026. Operated by Kirksville Kiwanis Club. Start date corrected from prior run (prior entry started June 28, 0 days out).

venue: Princeton Square (east side in front of Mercer County Senior Center)
address: 110 N Broadway area, Princeton Square, Princeton, MO 64673
audience: All ages; held directly in front of and in partnership with the Mercer County Senior Center, attracting significant senior attendance
tabling_details: FREE vendor spaces. Vendors set up on the east side of the Princeton Square in front of the Senior Center, Saturdays 8am-noon. No charge to vendors. Activities planned for each Saturday.
notes: Saturdays 8am-noon in front of Mercer County Senior Center. First Saturday in >=21-day window: July 25, 2026. Runs through fall. Contact Princeton Chamber at 660-748-4009. Senior Center partnership confirmed. Start date corrected from prior run (prior entry started June 28, 0 days out).

audience: General community including seniors/families; county fair with broad demographic attendance
tabling_details: Commercial vendor booths available; contact Committee Chair Christy Dempsey at 573-213-9876 for booth space. Fair features vendors, food, livestock, live music, rodeo, and more.
cost: Adult admission $20-$25 depending on day; vendor booth fees by application
notes: Annual county fair running July 28-August 1, 2026. Commercial vendor/booth space available by contacting Christy Dempsey. Fair includes vendors, food, 4-H/livestock, live music, truck/tractor pull, demolition derby, rodeo.

address: Flower City Park, N Breckenridge Ave, Palmyra, MO 63461
audience: General public / seniors (Senior Night debut in 2026; senior discounts all week)
tabling_details: Vendor/exhibitor/concessionaire spaces available both outdoor and indoor. Contact office.mcfg@gmail.com for inquiries. Registration via FairEntry at marioncounty.fairentry.com.
cost: General admission tickets; BOGO deal for seniors on Senior Night ($8 for two tickets)
notes: Senior Night making its debut at the Marion County Fair in 2026 with special senior discounts all week. Fair features livestock shows, horse shows, dog shows, arts and crafts exhibits, evening performances, rodeos, merchant vendors, senior citizens day, veterans day, kids days, rides, and fair food.

audience: General public; Senior Night (new for 2026) specifically targets 65+ with BOGO ticket deal; seniors receive discount all week
tabling_details: Indoor air-conditioned Marketplace with 10x10 booths available for commercial vendors/exhibitors. Email office.mcfg@gmail.com for vendor applications. Senior Night debuts in 2026 with BOGO admission deal for seniors.
notes: Senior Night brand new for 2026 — exclusive BOGO deal (2 tickets for $8) on one evening during fair week. Fair runs August 1-8, 2026 at Flower City Park, Palmyra, MO 63461. Sub-event row per schema rules for senior-specific programming.

audience: General community including seniors; traditional small-town county fair draws all ages with heavy senior attendance
tabling_details: Search results confirm the fair includes vendors and activities throughout the multi-day event; contact fair organizers for booth applications
notes: confirmed
column22: Annual Higbee Fair in Randolph County includes carnival, musical entertainment, and vendors. Dates August 13-15, 2026 confirmed in search results. Higbee is a city in Randolph County, MO.

audience: All ages; large community festival draws seniors/65+ along with families and general public
tabling_details: Festival includes craft and retail vendor booths open 8 a.m.-4 p.m. on Saturday. Vendor applications accepted; general vendor and food vendor categories available. Contact Mexico Jaycees at mexicomojaycees@gmail.com for vendor application.
cost: Free admission for attendees; vendor booth fees apply
notes: Annual festival hosted by Mexico Jaycees. Includes parade, live music, car show, carnival, beer garden, cornhole tournament, craft and retail vendors, and food. August 20-22 dates reported by multiple sources for 2026.

audience: All ages; broad community attendance including seniors/65+ population in Sullivan County
tabling_details: County fair with commercial vendor exhibitor opportunities; contact fair organizers via sullivancountyfair.org or Sullivan County Expo Center & Fairgrounds Facebook page for vendor/exhibitor booth details.
notes: confirmed
column22: 2026 Sullivan County Fair dates confirmed as September 2-6 via official fair website schedule page. Annual county fair with livestock shows, entertainment, youth exhibits, and food vendors. County fair format typically includes commercial vendor building.

audience: General community; multi-generational; craft shows and community festivals in small rural towns draw a high proportion of seniors 65+
tabling_details: Event explicitly features Courtyard Vendors of all kinds and Food Vendors. Contact: 660-216-1065 or KCcornfest@gmail.com or via Facebook page Knox County Corn Festival - Official.
notes: confirmed
column22: Annual festival held the first weekend after Labor Day in Edina, MO. Features craft show, baby show, talent contest, car show, small kids rides, basketball tournament, daily entertainment, raffle prizes, and local group-sponsored meals. 2026 dates Sept 4-6 confirmed by festivalnet.com and The Edina Sentinel.

audience: General public; artisan/craft/antique dealer festival with mixed-age audience; 55+ crowd well represented at artisan and antique-dealer events of this type
tabling_details: Mainstreet Macon Downtown Association actively recruits artisan, crafter, and specialty vendors; call 660-385-2811 for vendor registration; hosted by Macon Area Chamber of Commerce
notes: September 5, 2026 date confirmed via multiple search results. Event features high-end visual arts, quality crafters, antique dealers, live musical entertainment, food, and wine on historic downtown square. Vendor recruitment confirmed via centralmoinfo.com.

audience: General county fair audience; attracts broad community including large senior population; includes livestock shows, 4-H, demolition derby, carnival, commercial booths, baby show, entertainment concerts
tabling_details: Indoor commercial booths available in sizes from 10 ft to 30 ft wide. Open to local businesses, commercial goods, handcrafted wares, government agencies, political parties and candidates, and civic organizations. Priority given to returning vendors. Apply via putnamcountyfairunionvillemo.com.
cost: Daily entry $25 at gate; season pass $50 (through September 4) or $55 at gate; kids 12 and under free
notes: Annual county fair first Tuesday-Saturday following Labor Day. 2026 Labor Day = Sept 7; fair runs Sept 8-12. Confirmed 2026 entertainers: Rhonda Vincent (Sept 9) and Joe Nichols (Sept 12, 7:30 PM). Senior Citizens Day Wednesday Sept 9 with free admission for seniors 62+.

event_name: Putnam County Fair - Senior Citizens Day
audience: General fair audience with dedicated Senior Citizens Day; seniors 62+ admitted free; senior activities 9am-1pm in Entertainment Tent with music 10am-1pm
tabling_details: Indoor commercial booths available throughout the fair week (10-30 ft wide); booth types include local businesses, civic organizations, government agencies, and commercial vendors. Fair organizers note that commercial exhibitors are encouraged to staff their booths on Senior Citizens Day.
cost: Seniors 62+ free on Senior Citizens Day; general daily entry $25; season pass $50-$55
notes: Senior Citizens Day held annually on Wednesday of fair week. 2026 fair runs Sept 8-12; Senior Day falls September 9. Rhonda Vincent performs Sept 9 at 7 PM. Sub-event row per schema rules for senior-specific programming.

audience: General public including seniors; community festival with parade, craft, and food vendor booths
tabling_details: Festival historically features handmade goods, art, clothing, jewelry, direct sales vendors, food trucks; vendor/craft booth applications available through organizers
notes: confirmed
column22: Annual September festival held the second weekend of September in Keytesville, MO (Chariton County). Second weekend of September 2026 = September 12-13. Confirm exact dates with organizers before planning tabling.

audience: Community members including seniors; free health screenings and demonstrations and health resources attract older adults
tabling_details: Formal exhibitor registration process; each exhibitor provided 8-foot table with tablecloth and two chairs. 50+ local and national exhibitors participated in 2025 event. Contact kirksvilleexpo@atsu.edu to register as exhibitor.
notes: Annual event hosted by A.T. Still University. 2025 event was September 6 (9am-3pm); 2026 date of September 12 identified in search results. Features free health screenings (dental, skin, oral cancer, hearing), food trucks, demonstrations, raffles, children's fun zone.

audience: Vintage/antique shoppers skewing 45+/senior; event draws ~15,000 attendees
tabling_details: 100+ vendor booths; 2026 vendor application form available at form.jotform.com/253515967641162; contact Moberly Area Chamber of Commerce at chamber@moberlychamber.com
notes: One of the largest vintage markets in the Midwest, featuring vintage, antique, handmade, repurposed, and specialty items. Runs 9 am - 5 pm both days. Vendor application form confirmed for 2026 season. Hosted by Moberly Area Chamber of Commerce.

audience: General community including seniors; annual downtown street festival drawing broad community attendance
tabling_details: Craft and food vendor booths available for $35 registration fee through the Bowling Green MO Chamber of Commerce. Both seller (vendor) and non-seller (exhibitor) spots available, including civic organizations and businesses. Food vendors must comply with Pike County Health Department regulations and provide liability insurance.
contact_name: Bowling Green Missouri Chamber of Commerce
notes: Annual festival held on the second Saturday in September on the downtown square. Features local food and craft vendors, car/truck/tractor show (Bowling Green Lions Club), parade, live music, tours of Champ Clark's Honey Shuck. Vendor registration $35. Event date September 12, 2026.

audience: General public / all ages; craft barn, flea market, rodeo, and mule events strongly attract 55+/65+ crowd
tabling_details: Craft vendor booths available in a large craft barn; approximately 25 businesses display and sell items (quilts, rugs, wood crafts, metal art, baskets, ceramics, jewelry, homemade soap, etc.). Flea market space also available. Contact festival organizer for vendor applications.
cost: Vendor fees vary; contact organizer for details
notes: Annual festival since 1986, held the 3rd full weekend of September. 2025 dates were Sept 18-20; 2026 dates estimated Sept 17-20. Features mule parade, mule shows, craft barn with ~25 vendors, flea market, rodeo, trail rides, music, and food. Craft barn open Fri-Sun from 8am.

audience: General community including seniors; annual fall festival drawing crowds from across the region to Princeton and Calamity Jane's birthplace
tabling_details: Food and craft vendors confirmed as part of the event. West side of the square reserved for Community Market vendors; remainder of square open for garage sale booths and other vendors. Contact Princeton Chamber of Commerce at 660-748-4009 for vendor information.
notes: September 18-20, 2026 confirmed by multiple sources. Theme: '250 Years of Freedom.' Grand Marshalls: Shane and Marcia Cox. Features food and craft vendors, blacksmith and beekeeping demonstrations, pony rides, petting zoo, face painting, parade, car show.

address: Glascock's Landing, historic downtown Hannibal, MO
audience: General public; seniors attend; broad community event with arts/crafts, farmers market, classic car cruise, pumpkin carving
tabling_details: Event includes vendors, farmers market, arts and crafts. Festival benefits the Rotary Club of Hannibal. Vendor participation implied by event format.
notes: 11th Annual Harvest Hootenanny. Includes live music, bands, beer, food, vendors, petting zoo, bounce houses, pony rides, classic car cruise, farmers market, arts and crafts, pumpkin carving. Benefits Rotary Club of Hannibal. Dates confirmed September 18-19, 2026.

event_name: Red Barn Arts and Crafts Festival
audience: General public including seniors; arts and crafts festivals historically draw strong 55+ attendance
tabling_details: Juried exhibitor application process; 100+ booths; applications for exhibitors and food vendors open online through Kirksville Arts Association. 52nd annual event in 2026.
notes: 52nd annual Red Barn Arts and Crafts Festival organized by Kirksville Arts Association. Juried event with standards for design, originality, and execution. Attracts nearly 10,000 attendees to 100+ booths on the downtown Courthouse Square. September 19 date confirmed in 2026 Missouri Festival Guide and search results.

audience: General community including seniors; chamber-organized community festival draws all ages
tabling_details: Monroe City Chamber of Commerce organizes the event; craft and commercial vendor booths expected; contact Monroe City Chamber at (573) 735-4585 for vendor registration.
notes: October 3, 2026 date confirmed in 2026 Missouri Festival Guide. Monroe City is the largest city in Monroe County, MO. Chamber-organized festivals typically include craft and commercial vendor booths. Call (573) 735-4585 to verify vendor application details before booking.

audience: Community members of all ages; Alzheimer's disease disproportionately affects 65+; walkers, caregivers, and sponsor tables all present
tabling_details: Sponsorship tables available as part of standard Walk to End Alzheimer's format. Sponsor tables at the walk site are a standard part of sponsorship packages. Contact Alzheimer's Association Greater Missouri Chapter at 314-432-3422 for sponsorship opportunities.
cost: Free to walk; sponsorship packages available
notes: October 10, 2026 date confirmed via official 2026 Walk to End Alzheimer's Kirksville event registration page (act.alz.org fr_id=19734). Venue not yet listed publicly; contact Alzheimer's Association Greater Missouri Chapter for location details.
